post.content:
I think this can be solved easily by keeping two variables
ck, cv
cummulative key sum and value sum respectively
when adding a new key add m[k - ck] = v - cv
while getting a value search return m[k - ck] + cv
when incrementing key do ck += inc
when incrementing value do cv += inc

All these comments suggest using an offset but that doesn\u2019t work, because the offset should only be applied to the elements in the map at the time or the addToX call. If insert gets called later, the addToX should not influence this new entry. Simply using offset variables doesn\u2019t work here.
This is from CodeSignal practice test.
I think you can reuse ideas from https://leetcode.com/problems/fancy-sequence/

import collections
def solution(queryType, query):
    
    lookup = collections.defaultdict(int)
    getSum = 0
    
    ck, cv = 0, 0
    
    for i in range(len(queryType)):
        op, arr = queryType[i], query[i]
        if op == "insert":
            x, y = arr[0], arr[1]
            lookup[x - ck] = y - cv
        elif op == "get":
            x = arr[0]
            vl = lookup[x-ck] + cv
            getSum += vl
        elif op == "addToKey":
            x = arr[0]
            ck += x
        else:
            y = arr[0]
            cv += y
        
    return getSum
